config._comment_tasks: Number of parallel Kafka Connect worker tasks for this connector
config._comment_class: The Iceberg Sink Connector class that writes Kafka records into Iceberg tables
config._comment_topics: Kafka topic to consume from — Debezium produces CDC events here from postgres.public.bookings
config._comment_tables: Target Iceberg table in the form <namespace>.<table>
config._comment_auto_create: Automatically create the Iceberg table if it does not exist yet
config._comment_evolve_schema: Automatically add new columns to the Iceberg table when the source schema changes
config._comment_catalog_impl: Use Nessie as the Iceberg catalog (git-like versioned catalog)
config._comment_warehouse: Root S3 path where Iceberg data and metadata files are stored
config._comment_io_impl: Use the S3 file I/O implementation to read/write Iceberg files
config._comment_path_style: Force path-style S3 URLs (required for MinIO, which does not use virtual-hosted-style)
config._comment_region: AWS/MinIO region — must match across all clients for SigV4 request signing
config._comment_key_converter: Deserialize Kafka message keys as JSON
config._comment_key_schema: Expect the key JSON to include an embedded schema envelope (Debezium default)
config._comment_value_converter: Deserialize Kafka message values as JSON
config._comment_value_schema: Expect the value JSON to include an embedded schema envelope (Debezium default)
config._comment_transforms: Named list of SMTs (Single Message Transforms) to apply in order
config._comment_unwrap_type: ExtractNewRecordState: strips the Debezium envelope and keeps only the 'after' image of the row
config._comment_tombstones: Keep tombstone records (deletion marker: null-value messages produced by Debezium for DELETEs) instead of dropping them
config._comment_delete_mode: drop = discard DELETE events entirely; they will not be written to Iceberg
config._comment_commit_interval: How often (ms) the connector commits a new Iceberg snapshot — up to 5s of latency before data is visible
config._comment_idempotence: Disable Kafka producer idempotence (simplifies setup; enable in production for exactly-once guarantees)
config._comment_txn_id: No transactional producer ID — required when idempotence is disabled
config._comment_exactly_once_source: Disable exactly-once support on the source side
config._comment_exactly_once_sink: Disable exactly-once support on the sink side
